english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Hebrews

verses.latin_vulgate: Est autem fides sperandarum substantia rerum, argumentum non apparentium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕστιν δὲ πίστις ἐλπιζομένων ὑπόστασις πραγμάτων ἔλεγχος οὐ βλεπομένων
verses.king_james: Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.

verses.latin_vulgate: In hac enim testimonium consecuti sunt senes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ταύτῃ γὰρ ἐμαρτυρήθησαν οἱ πρεσβύτεροι
verses.king_james: For by it the elders obtained a good report.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide intelligimus aptata esse sæcula verbo Dei: ut ex invisibilibus visibilia fierent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει νοοῦμεν κατηρτίσθαι τοὺς αἰῶνας ῥήματι θεοῦ εἰς τὸ μὴ ἐκ φαινομένων τά βλεπόμενα γεγονέναι
verses.king_james: Through faith we understand that the worlds were framed by the word of God, so that things which are seen were not made of things which do appear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide plurimam hostiam Abel, quam Cain, obtulit Deo, per quam testimonium consecutus est esse justus, testimonium perhibente muneribus ejus Deo, et per illam defunctus adhuc loquitur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει πλείονα θυσίαν ἅβελ παρὰ κάϊν προσήνεγκεν τῷ θεῷ δι᾽ ἡς ἐμαρτυρήθη εἶναι δίκαιος μαρτυροῦντος ἐπὶ τοῖς δώροις αὐτοῦ τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ δι᾽ αὐτῆς ἀποθανὼν ἔτι λαλεῖται
verses.king_james: By faith Abel offered unto God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, by which he obtained witness that he was righteous, God testifying of his gifts: and by it he being dead yet speaketh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide Henoch translatus est ne videret mortem, et non inveniebatur, quia transtulit illum Deus: ante translationem enim testimonium habuit placuisse Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει ἑνὼχ μετετέθη τοῦ μὴ ἰδεῖν θάνατον καὶ οὐχ εὑρίσκετο διότι μετέθηκεν αὐτὸν ὁ θεός πρὸ γὰρ τῆς μεταθέσεως αὐτοῦ μεμαρτύρηται εὐηρεστηκέναι τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: By faith Enoch was translated that he should not see death; and was not found, because God had translated him: for before his translation he had this testimony, that he pleased God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sine fide autem impossibile est placere Deo. Credere enim oportet accedentem ad Deum quia est, et inquirentibus se remunerator sit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χωρὶς δὲ πίστεως ἀδύνατον εὐαρεστῆσαι πιστεῦσαι γὰρ δεῖ τὸν προσερχόμενον τῷ θεῷ ὅτι ἔστιν καὶ τοῖς ἐκζητοῦσιν αὐτὸν μισθαποδότης γίνεται
verses.king_james: But without faith *it is* impossible to please *him*: for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and *that* he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide Noë responso accepto de iis quæ adhuc non videbantur, metuens aptavit arcam in salutem domus suæ, per quam damnavit mundum: et justitiæ, quæ per fidem est, hæres est institutus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει χρηματισθεὶς νῶε περὶ τῶν μηδέπω βλεπομένων εὐλαβηθεὶς κατεσκεύασεν κιβωτὸν εἰς σωτηρίαν τοῦ οἴκου αὐτοῦ δι᾽ ἡς κατέκρινεν τὸν κόσμον καὶ τῆς κατὰ πίστιν δικαιοσύνης ἐγένετο κληρονόμος
verses.king_james: By faith Noah, being warned of God of things not seen as yet, moved with fear, prepared an ark to the saving of his house; by the which he condemned the world, and became heir of the righteousness which is by faith.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide qui vocatur Abraham obedivit in locum exire, quem accepturus erat in hæreditatem: et exiit, nesciens quo iret.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει καλούμενος ἀβραὰμ ὑπήκουσεν ἐξελθεῖν εἰς τὸν τόπον ὃν ἤμελλεν λαμβάνειν εἰς κληρονομίαν καὶ ἐξῆλθεν μὴ ἐπιστάμενος ποῦ ἔρχεται
verses.king_james: By faith Abraham, when he was called to go out into a place which he should after receive for an inheritance, obeyed; and he went out, not knowing whither he went.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide demoratus est in terra repromissionis, tamquam in aliena, in casulis habitando cum Isaac et Jacob cohæredibus repromissionis ejusdem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει παρῴκησεν εἰς τὴν γῆν τῆς ἐπαγγελίας ὡς ἀλλοτρίαν ἐν σκηναῖς κατοικήσας μετὰ ἰσαὰκ καὶ ἰακὼβ τῶν συγκληρονόμων τῆς ἐπαγγελίας τῆς αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: By faith he sojourned in the land of promise, as *in* a strange country, dwelling in tabernacles with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise:

verses.latin_vulgate: Exspectabat enim fundamenta habentem civitatem: cujus artifex et conditor Deus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξεδέχετο γὰρ τὴν τοὺς θεμελίους ἔχουσαν πόλιν ἡς τεχνίτης καὶ δημιουργὸς ὁ θεός
verses.king_james: For he looked for a city which hath foundations, whose builder and maker *is* God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide et ipsa Sara sterilis virtutem in conceptionem seminis accepit, etiam præter tempus ætatis: quoniam fidelem credidit esse eum qui repromiserat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει καὶ αὐτὴ σάῤῥα δύναμιν εἰς καταβολὴν σπέρματος ἔλαβεν καὶ παρὰ καιρὸν ἡλικίας ἔτεκεν ἐπεὶ πιστὸν ἡγήσατο τὸν ἐπαγγειλάμενον
verses.king_james: Through faith also Sara herself received strength to conceive seed, and was delivered of a child when she was past age, because she judged him faithful who had promised.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod et ab uno orti sunt (et hoc emortuo) tamquam sidera cæli in multitudinem, et sicut arena, quæ est ad oram maris, innumerabilis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ καὶ ἀφ᾽ ἑνὸς ἐγεννήθησαν καὶ ταῦτα νενεκρωμένου καθὼς τὰ ἄστρα τοῦ οὐρανοῦ τῷ πλήθει καὶ ὡσεὶ ἄμμος ἡ παρὰ τὸ χεῖλος τῆς θαλάσσης ἡ ἀναρίθμητος
verses.king_james: Therefore sprang there even of one, and him as good as dead, *so many* as the stars of the sky in multitude, and as the sand which is by the sea shore innumerable.

verses.latin_vulgate: Juxta fidem defuncti sunt omnes isti, non acceptis repromissionibus, sed a longe eas aspicientes, et salutantes, et confitentes quia peregrini et hospites sunt super terram.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατὰ πίστιν ἀπέθανον οὗτοι πάντες μὴ λαβόντες τὰς ἐπαγγελίας ἀλλὰ πόῤῥωθεν αὐτὰς ἰδόντες καὶ πεισθέντες καὶ ἀσπασάμενοι καὶ ὁμολογήσαντες ὅτι ξένοι καὶ παρεπίδημοί εἰσιν ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς
verses.king_james: These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them afar off, and were persuaded of *them*, and embraced *them*, and confessed that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui enim hæc dicunt, significant se patriam inquirere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ γὰρ τοιαῦτα λέγοντες ἐμφανίζουσιν ὅτι πατρίδα ἐπιζητοῦσιν
verses.king_james: For they that say such things declare plainly that they seek a country.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si quidem ipsius meminissent de qua exierunt, habebant utique tempus revertendi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰ μὲν ἐκείνης ἐμνημόνευον ἀφ᾽ ἡς ἐξῆλθον εἶχον ἂν καιρὸν ἀνακάμψαι
verses.king_james: And truly, if they had been mindful of that *country* from whence they came out, they might have had opportunity to have returned.

verses.latin_vulgate: nunc autem meliorem appetunt, id est, cælestem. Ideo non confunditur Deus vocari Deus eorum: paravit enim illis civitatem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νυνὶ δὲ κρείττονος ὀρέγονται τουτέστιν ἐπουρανίου διὸ οὐκ ἐπαισχύνεται αὐτοὺς ὁ θεὸς θεὸς ἐπικαλεῖσθαι αὐτῶν ἡτοίμασεν γὰρ αὐτοῖς πόλιν
verses.king_james: But now they desire a better *country*, that is, an heavenly: wherefore God is not ashamed to be called their God: for he hath prepared for them a city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide obtulit Abraham Isaac, cum tentaretur, et unigenitum offerebat, qui susceperat repromissiones:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει προσενήνοχεν ἀβραὰμ τὸν ἰσαὰκ πειραζόμενος καὶ τὸν μονογενῆ προσέφερεν ὁ τὰς ἐπαγγελίας ἀναδεξάμενος
verses.king_james: By faith Abraham, when he was tried, offered up Isaac: and he that had received the promises offered up his only begotten *son*,

verses.latin_vulgate: ad quem dictum est: Quia in Isaac vocabitur tibi semen:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πρὸς ὃν ἐλαλήθη ὅτι ἐν ἰσαὰκ κληθήσεταί σοι σπέρμα
verses.king_james: Of whom it was said, That in Isaac shall thy seed be called:

verses.latin_vulgate: arbitrans quia et a mortuis suscitare potens est Deus: unde eum et in parabolam accepit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λογισάμενος ὅτι καὶ ἐκ νεκρῶν ἐγείρειν δυνατὸς ὁ θεός ὅθεν αὐτὸν καὶ ἐν παραβολῇ ἐκομίσατο
verses.king_james: Accounting that God *was* able to raise *him* up, even from the dead; from whence also he received him in a figure.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide et de futuris benedixit Isaac Jacob et Esau.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει περὶ μελλόντων εὐλόγησεν ἰσαὰκ τὸν ἰακὼβ καὶ τὸν ἠσαῦ
verses.king_james: By faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things to come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide Jacob, moriens, singulos filiorum Joseph benedixit: et adoravit fastigium virgæ ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει ἰακὼβ ἀποθνῄσκων ἕκαστον τῶν υἱῶν ἰωσὴφ εὐλόγησεν καὶ προσεκύνησεν ἐπὶ τὸ ἄκρον τῆς ῥάβδου αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: By faith Jacob, when he was a dying, blessed both the sons of Joseph; and worshipped, *leaning* upon the top of his staff.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide Joseph, moriens, de profectione filiorum Israël memoratus est, et de ossibus suis mandavit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει ἰωσὴφ τελευτῶν περὶ τῆς ἐξόδου τῶν υἱῶν ἰσραὴλ ἐμνημόνευσεν καὶ περὶ τῶν ὀστέων αὐτοῦ ἐνετείλατο
verses.king_james: By faith Joseph, when he died, made mention of the departing of the children of Israel; and gave commandment concerning his bones.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide Moyses, natus, occultatus est mensibus tribus a parentibus suis, eo quod vidissent elegantem infantem, et non timuerunt regis edictum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει μωσῆς γεννηθεὶς ἐκρύβη τρίμηνον ὑπὸ τῶν πατέρων αὐτοῦ διότι εἶδον ἀστεῖον τὸ παιδίον καὶ οὐκ ἐφοβήθησαν τὸ διάταγμα τοῦ βασιλέως
verses.king_james: By faith Moses, when he was born, was hid three months of his parents, because they saw *he was* a proper child; and they were not afraid of the king's commandment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide Moyses grandis factus negavit se esse filium filiæ Pharaonis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει μωσῆς μέγας γενόμενος ἠρνήσατο λέγεσθαι υἱὸς θυγατρὸς φαραώ
verses.king_james: By faith Moses, when he was come to years, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh's daughter;

verses.latin_vulgate: magis eligens affligi cum populo Dei, quam temporalis peccati habere jucunditatem,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μᾶλλον ἑλόμενος συγκακουχεῖσθαι τῷ λαῷ τοῦ θεοῦ ἢ πρόσκαιρον ἔχειν ἁμαρτίας ἀπόλαυσιν
verses.king_james: Choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God, than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season;

verses.latin_vulgate: majores divitias æstimans thesauro Ægyptiorum, improperium Christi: aspiciebat enim in remunerationem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μείζονα πλοῦτον ἡγησάμενος τῶν ἐν αἰγύπτῳ θησαυρῶν τὸν ὀνειδισμὸν τοῦ χριστοῦ ἀπέβλεπεν γὰρ εἰς τὴν μισθαποδοσίαν
verses.king_james: Esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures in Egypt: for he had respect unto the recompence of the reward.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide reliquit Ægyptum, non veritus animositatem regis: invisibilem enim tamquam videns sustinuit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει κατέλιπεν αἴγυπτον μὴ φοβηθεὶς τὸν θυμὸν τοῦ βασιλέως τὸν γὰρ ἀόρατον ὡς ὁρῶν ἐκαρτέρησεν
verses.king_james: By faith he forsook Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king: for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide celebravit Pascha, et sanguinis effusionem: ne qui vastabat primitiva, tangeret eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει πεποίηκεν τὸ πάσχα καὶ τὴν πρόσχυσιν τοῦ αἵματος ἵνα μὴ ὁ ὀλοθρεύων τὰ πρωτότοκα θίγῃ αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Through faith he kept the passover, and the sprinkling of blood, lest he that destroyed the firstborn should touch them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide transierunt mare Rubrum tamquam per aridam terram: quod experti Ægyptii, devorati sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει διέβησαν τὴν ἐρυθρὰν θάλασσαν ὡς διὰ ξηρᾶς ἡς πεῖραν λαβόντες οἱ αἰγύπτιοι κατεπόθησαν
verses.king_james: By faith they passed through the Red sea as by dry *land*: which the Egyptians assaying to do were drowned.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide muri Jericho corruerunt, circuitu dierum septem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει τὰ τείχη ἰεριχὼ ἔπεσεν κυκλωθέντα ἐπὶ ἑπτὰ ἡμέρας
verses.king_james: By faith the walls of Jericho fell down, after they were compassed about seven days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fide Rahab meretrix non periit cum incredulis, excipiens exploratores cum pace.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πίστει ῥαὰβ ἡ πόρνη οὐ συναπώλετο τοῖς ἀπειθήσασιν δεξαμένη τοὺς κατασκόπους μετ᾽ εἰρήνης
verses.king_james: By faith the harlot Rahab perished not with them that believed not, when she had received the spies with peace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quid adhuc dicam? deficiet enim me tempus enarrantem de Gedeon, Barac, Samson, Jephte, David, Samuel, et prophetis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τί ἔτι λέγω ἐπιλείψει γὰρ με διηγούμενον ὁ χρόνος περὶ γεδεών βαράκ τε καὶ σαμψών καὶ ἰεφθάε δαβίδ τε καὶ σαμουὴλ καὶ τῶν προφητῶν
verses.king_james: And what shall I more say? for the time would fail me to tell of Gedeon, and *of* Barak, and *of* Samson, and *of* Jephthae; *of* David also, and Samuel, and *of* the prophets:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui per fidem vicerunt regna, operati sunt justitiam, adepti sunt repromissiones, obturaverunt ora leonum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἳ διὰ πίστεως κατηγωνίσαντο βασιλείας εἰργάσαντο δικαιοσύνην ἐπέτυχον ἐπαγγελιῶν ἔφραξαν στόματα λεόντων
verses.king_james: Who through faith subdued kingdoms, wrought righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,

verses.latin_vulgate: extinxerunt impetum ignis, effugerunt aciem gladii, convaluerunt de infirmitate, fortes facti sunt in bello, castra verterunt exterorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔσβεσαν δύναμιν πυρός ἔφυγον στόματα μαχαίρας ἐνεδυναμώθησαν ἀπὸ ἀσθενείας ἐγενήθησαν ἰσχυροὶ ἐν πολέμῳ παρεμβολὰς ἔκλιναν ἀλλοτρίων
verses.king_james: Quenched the violence of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, out of weakness were made strong, waxed valiant in fight, turned to flight the armies of the aliens.

verses.latin_vulgate: acceperunt mulieres de resurrectione mortuos suos: alii autem distenti sunt non suscipientes redemptionem ut meliorem invenirent resurrectionem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλαβον γυναῖκες ἐξ ἀναστάσεως τοὺς νεκροὺς αὐτῶν ἄλλοι δὲ ἐτυμπανίσθησαν οὐ προσδεξάμενοι τὴν ἀπολύτρωσιν ἵνα κρείττονος ἀναστάσεως τύχωσιν
verses.king_james: Women received their dead raised to life again: and others were tortured, not accepting deliverance; that they might obtain a better resurrection:

verses.latin_vulgate: Alii vero ludibria, et verbera experti, insuper et vincula, et carceres:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕτεροι δὲ ἐμπαιγμῶν καὶ μαστίγων πεῖραν ἔλαβον ἔτι δὲ δεσμῶν καὶ φυλακῆς
verses.king_james: And others had trial of *cruel* mockings and scourgings, yea, moreover of bonds and imprisonment:

verses.latin_vulgate: lapidati sunt, secti sunt, tentati sunt, in occisione gladii mortui sunt, circuierunt in melotis, in pellibus caprinis, egentes, angustiati, afflicti:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλιθάσθησαν ἐπρίσθησαν ἐπειράσθησαν ἐν φόνῳ μαχαίρας ἀπέθανον περιῆλθον ἐν μηλωταῖς ἐν αἰγείοις δέρμασιν ὑστερούμενοι θλιβόμενοι κακουχούμενοι
verses.king_james: They were stoned, they were sawn asunder, were tempted, were slain with the sword: they wandered about in sheepskins and goatskins; being destitute, afflicted, tormented;

verses.latin_vulgate: quibus dignus non erat mundus: in solitudinibus errantes, in montibus, in speluncis, et in cavernis terræ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὧν οὐκ ἦν ἄξιος ὁ κόσμος ἐν ἐρημίαις πλανώμενοι καὶ ὄρεσιν καὶ σπηλαίοις καὶ ταῖς ὀπαῖς τῆς γῆς
verses.king_james: (Of whom the world was not worthy:) they wandered in deserts, and *in* mountains, and *in* dens and caves of the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hi omnes testimonio fidei probati, non acceperunt repromissionem,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὗτοι πάντες μαρτυρηθέντες διὰ τῆς πίστεως οὐκ ἐκομίσαντο τὴν ἐπαγγελίαν
verses.king_james: And these all, having obtained a good report through faith, received not the promise:

verses.latin_vulgate: Deo pro nobis melius aliquid providente, ut non sine nobis consummarentur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ θεοῦ περὶ ἡμῶν κρεῖττόν τι προβλεψαμένου ἵνα μὴ χωρὶς ἡμῶν τελειωθῶσιν
verses.king_james: God having provided some better thing for us, that they without us should not be made perfect.
